proverb_en: Better once in heaven than ten times at the gate.

He who has money has capers.
Don’t sell the bearskin before the bear is dead.

hearing is untrustworthy, seeing is believing

Who would win, must learn to bear.
Honour the tree that gives you shelter.
Don’t reckon your eggs before they are laid.
On a fool’s beard all learn to shave.

We ate, drank and had fun. Calculated
notes: Ate, drank, made merry. Counted, and shed tears

proverb_en: Justice, but not in my own house.
Better bend than break.
Big fish spring out of the kettle.
Every man his own is not too much.
Who does too much often does little.
Where money and counsel are wanting, it is best not to make war.
It is better to make conditions in the bush than in prison.
If I get fruit in my hand, why do I want to cling 2
notes: Why do we need a hooked pole when the fruit is already in our hand

proverb_en: practice makes perfect
make it do or do without
When the cat’s away the mice will play.
mind over matter
He who sows brambles reaps thorns.

notes: passage of time cannot legitimize an unjust act

proverb_en: He who has left a rogue behind him, has made a good day’s journey.
notes: the relief of parting ways with untrustworthy or dishonest people

proverb_en: Fear is a great inventor.
What the hell is not joking
notes: What doesn't the devil joke about? / anything is possible

proverb_en: Who has something is something.

notes: being cheap or hesitant to spend on essential resources initially often results in much greater expenses

proverb_en: He who buys what he don’t want, will soon sell what he does want.
Chastise a good child, that it may not grow bad, and a bad one, that it may not grow worse.
Swakhat Salil drowned and died Shyama Dosha Karo Naigo Ma
notes: I drown in the water of my own well; nobody else is to blame, Mother

proverb_en: you are eating from the bowl and looking at the pot
I will do what I can, and a little less, to be able to continue at it.
Take advice of a red-bearded man, and be gone.
